description: ## Component: hx-checkbox-group (NEW) **Tier 1 — Universal Foundation | Industry Consensus: 4/6 libraries** Groups related checkboxes under shared label, validation, and form integration. Multi-value field pattern for Drupal. ### ADR-001 Classification: Hybrid (Form Component) Container component. Properties control group-level validation. Slots accept hx-checkbox children from Drupal TWIG templates. ### API Design **Properties:** - `name`: string (shared name for all children) - `label`: string (group label) - `required`: boolean (at least one must be checked) - `error`: string - `orientation`: horizontal | vertical **Slots:** - Default slot: hx-checkbox children - `label`: Group label (rich HTML) - `help`: Group-level help text - `error`: Group-level error **CSS Parts:** `group`, `label`, `help-text`, `error-message` **Events:** `hx-change` (composed: true, detail: { values: string[] }) ### Drupal Integration ```twig {# Multi-value field from Drupal taxonomy #} {% for term in conditions_vocabulary %} {{ term.name }} {% endfor %} Select all that apply ``` Drupal controls which checkboxes appear (from taxonomy, entity references, etc.). Component handles layout, group validation, and form value collection. ### ElementInternals: Submits array of checked values ### Healthcare Use Cases - Pre-existing conditions selection - Symptoms checklist - Allergy selection - Insurance coverage types ### Acceptance Criteria 1. Groups checkboxes with shared label and validation 2. Required validation: at least one must be checked 3. Submits array of values via ElementInternals 4. Drupal renders checkbox children dynamically from taxonomy 5. Error state applies to group, not individual checkboxes 6. WCAG 2.1 AA: group role, describedby for help/error
title: T1-10: hx-checkbox-group — Grouped Checkboxes

Inline comments:

`@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-checkbox-group/hx-checkbox-group.stories.ts`:
- Line 279: The play functions use non-null assertions (e.g., passing
diabetesCheckbox! into clickCheckbox) which violate strict TypeScript; replace
these with proper runtime guards: when you call clickCheckbox or similar helpers
(reference clickCheckbox and variables like diabetesCheckbox) first query the
element, assert its existence with an if-check or throw a descriptive error (or
use test helpers like expect(element).toBeInTheDocument()), then call
clickCheckbox with the validated element; apply the same pattern to the other
occurrences noted in the file (the other query selector variables referenced at
lines 285, 362, 644–645, 769–770, 871, 889) so no call sites use the ! non-null
operator.
- Around line 18-26: Remove the duplicate argTypes.label entry that overwrites
the public property control (the second `argTypes.label` in the CSS Parts
section) so the Properties-section definition remains the authoritative one; if
you intended to document a CSS part, use a different argType key (e.g., `part: {
... }`) or merge the parts info without reusing `label`. Also remove all
non-null assertions used in the play functions (e.g., `diabetesCheckbox!` and
similar) and either type the variables correctly, use optional chaining (`?.`)
or null-checks before use so the code compiles under strict TypeScript (update
the play functions where variables like diabetesCheckbox, etc., are accessed).

In
`@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-checkbox-group/hx-checkbox-group.styles.ts`:
- Around line 9-79: The styles contain hardcoded fallback literals (e.g., 0.5,
0.75rem, `#343a40`, 500, 700) which violates the token-only rule; update the CSS
in selectors .fieldset, .fieldse

threadFeedback.agentReasoning: The bot flagged non-null assertions (`!`) in hx-checkbox-group.ts. Inspecting the current code, there are zero non-null assertions — all `!` characters are boolean negation operators (`!this.name`, `!(state instanceof FormData)`, `!!this.error`, `!this._hasErrorSlot`). The `@query` field uses `HTMLElement | null = null` with `?? undefined` nullish coalescing, not a `!` assertion. The bot appears to have reviewed an older version of the code before the `_itemsEl!: HTMLElement` was corrected to `_itemsEl: HTMLElement | null = null`. No action needed.

The bot claims `_syncCheckboxNames()` "only sets names on children with empty names, leaving existing child hx-checkboxes stale." This is factually incorrect. The current implementation at lines 164–169 does `cb.name = this.name` unconditionally for every child — there is no `if (!cb.name)` guard whatsoever. All children are always updated when `this.name` is non-empty. The bot's analysis is based on incorrect assumptions about the code, likely from a different version or hallucinated behavior.

The bot flagged non-null assertions in hx-checkbox-group.ts, but there are zero `!` non-null assertions in the current file. All `!` characters are boolean negation operators. The @query field had already been changed from `_itemsEl!: HTMLElement` to a nullable type in fea7e3a. No action needed.

The bot claimed `_syncCheckboxNames()` "only sets names on children with empty names." This is factually incorrect — the current code unconditionally sets `cb.name = this.name` for all children with no guard. The bot analyzed incorrect/stale code.

Real accessibility issue: `aria-describedby` needed to only reference `_errorId` when the error div is actually rendered in the shadow DOM. Fixed by using `!!this.error && !this._hasErrorSlot` as the condition (matching exactly when `id=${this._errorId}` is present in the DOM).
